e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not on the visa-free exempt list
e_visa.notes: Chad's e-Visa system via evisa.td is available to all foreigners not exempt from visa requirements. From 11 May 2026, all visa applications must be submitted exclusively online via evisa.td; visas issued outside the platform after 21 May 2026 are invalid at the border. Processing times: standard 10 business days, express 72 hours. Applications must be submitted at least 7 days before travel and no more than 60 days in advance (per the note circulaire; the FAQ may state 90 days). Extension is permitted only once for 15- and 30-day visas. Visa fees set by Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026. Diplomatic and courtesy visas remain handled by Chadian embassies and missions abroad. EXCEPTION: As of June 5, 2025, Chad has suspended visa issuance to US citizens (reciprocal response to the US travel ban on Chadian nationals). This suspension remained in effect as of June 2026 with no official announcement of lifting. Yellow fever vaccination certificate required for entry. All visitors must register with police within 72 hours of arrival.

visa_policy.notes: Chad's official e-visa platform (evisa.td) lists 27 nationalities exempt from visa requirements (including Chad itself). All other foreigners must apply for an e-Visa online. As of 11 May 2026, all new visa applications must be submitted exclusively via evisa.td; manual entry authorizations have been abolished. Legal basis: Décret n°2299/PR/PM/MSPI/2025 and Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026. Note: Chad suspended visa issuance to US citizens effective June 5, 2025 (reciprocal measure in response to the US travel ban on Chadian nationals). The US fully suspended visa issuance to Chadian nationals effective January 1, 2026. As of June 2026, neither suspension has been lifted per available information. The 'Congo' entry on the evisa.td exempt list refers to the Republic of the Congo (COG/Brazzaville); the Democratic Republic of Congo (COD/Kinshasa) is NOT on the list.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic passports issued to nationals of these 20 countries can visit Chad without a visa for up to 90 days. Source: Circulaire 002/MSPI/SG/2017, dated 8 August 2017, issued by Chad's Ministry of Security and Public Immigration (MSPI). Note: the Chad Embassy in Paris document (2019) confirms diplomatic AND service passport holders pay no visa fee ('Le visa est gratuit pour les détenteurs de Passeports Diplomatiques ou de Services'), but that document describes a fee waiver for a required visa, not a visa-free waiver — it is therefore NOT a separate service-passport visa-free rule. The visa-free waiver in Circulaire 002 names only diplomatic passports. The 20-country list is confirmed by the Wikipedia Visa policy of Chad article citing the same circular.

cbi.notes: No Citizenship-by-Investment program found for Chad on any official government source. No official CBI program exists as of June 2026.

agreements: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) — free movement of persons among member states (Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Republic of Congo, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon)

validation_notes: June 2026 re-validation: evisa.td portal re-fetched and confirmed 27-country visa-free list exactly matches prior record. No additions or removals detected.
Clarified 'Congo' entry: confirmed as Republic of Congo (COG/Brazzaville); DRC (COD) is NOT on the exempt list — added explicit note to the Congo entry and the policy notes field.

Confirmed Burkina Faso and Togo remain on the exempt list as of June 2026 — some non-official aggregators (Wikipedia) had noted these were removed when the e-visa system launched in December 2024, but the official evisa.td portal shows them present.

US citizen visa suspension updated: confirmed still in effect as of June 2026 (Chad reciprocal ban from June 5, 2025; US fully suspended visas to Chadian nationals from January 1, 2026). Neither ban has been publicly lifted. Note updated to reflect June 2026 status.
Added yellow fever vaccination certificate and mandatory 72-hour police registration requirements to e-visa notes — both verified from official evisa.td documentation.
max_stay_days for visa-free entries: official portal does not publish a duration. Secondary/aggregator sources suggest 90 days; this cannot be confirmed from primary source and remains null.
note-circulaire article confirms 60-day advance filing window (not 90 days) as the standard rule; corrected primary reference in e-visa notes.
Diplomatic passport conditional access list (Circulaire 002/MSPI/SG/2017, 20 countries, 90 days) confirmed unchanged by Wikipedia citation of same source.
No new CBI, RBI, or fast-track programs found on any official source.
CEMAC free-movement agreement entry confirmed accurate.
gaps: max_stay_days for visa-free nationalities not specified on the official evisa.td page; exact duration requires checking specific bilateral agreements or regulations not published on the portal
Exact e-visa fees/tariffs: Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026 is referenced but not published in full text on evisa.td
No official Chad government website (gouvernement.td, mae.td) was accessible with detailed visa policy pages beyond evisa.td
No official digital-nomad, skilled-worker, or investor-residence programs found on official sources
RBI/fast-track programs: no official Chad program found; omitted rather than recorded from non-official sources

US citizen visa suspension current status: Chad government has not published an official lifting notice; status as of June 2026 inferred from absence of any official announcement, not from a direct official statement

Conditional access — third-country visa/residence permit holders: aggregators mention no-visa rule for holders of valid US/Canada/UK/Schengen visa — NO official Chad government source (evisa.td, mae.td, MSPI) confirms this rule; NOT recorded per official-source-first policy
Conditional access — service passport holders: fee waiver confirmed for service passport holders by Embassy Paris document, but no official visa-free waiver for service passports found; not recorded

Diplomatic passport waiver — Circulaire 002/MSPI/SG/2017: original document URL is now 404; only an archived version (web.archive.org) remains accessible; the 20-country list is cited from this archived source
Transit visa policy: Wikipedia mentions 48-hour transit exemption; no confirmation on official evisa.td portal; not recorded
ID-card entry: Wikipedia mentions Cameroon, CAR, Republic of Congo, and Gabon nationals may enter using national ID cards instead of passports; not confirmed on official evisa.td portal; not recorded

outbound_official.note: no official Chad government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Chad's MFA website (diplomatie.gouv.td) is in maintenance mode and not publicly accessible as of June 2026. The Ministry of Public Security and Immigration (securite.gouv.td) does not publish an outbound visa-free list. No other official Chad government domain (.gouv.td, .td) was found to host a list of countries where Chadian ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa.
